title: HTTP Representations, Validators, Ranges, and File Transfer · Execution Trace: Download Accessibility
summary: A download endpoint guesses MIME types, buffers large bodies, ignores validators and Range, and treats Content-Disposition text as trusted HTML. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the download accessibility increment in HTTP file-representation and transfer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide accessible file names, types, sizes, progress, cancellation, error recovery, transcripts or alternatives, and keyboard-operable controls.
Stream through bounded buffers with cancellation, backpressure, body ownership, partial-write awareness, and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Download Accessibility · Theory Download Accessibility
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4670 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate conditional inclusive byte ranges and response-body cleanup; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide accessible file names, types, sizes, progress, cancellation, error recovery, transcripts or alternatives, and keyboard-operable controls.
